briefs.title: Council to vote on Burnaby's five-year financial plan covering 2026 to 2030
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would adopt the city's five-year financial plan for 2026–2030, outlining how the city plans to raise and spend money each year. If approved, the plan would set total 2026 spending at roughly $1.35 billion, with property taxes making up the largest share of revenue at 44.8% — and business properties carrying 37.1% of the property tax burden.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — approval will lock in property tax rates that directly affect your business costs.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Retail
